title: CSE 101 — Algorithms (UCSC, Winter 2020)
description: Interactive visualizations of the data structures and algorithms from Patricia Lopez's CSE 101. The N-Queens demo runs the original course C++ unmodified — Board.cpp + Queens.cpp from hw2/NQueens/ — compiled to WebAssembly with a thin step-by-step iterator shim. The AVL Wordrange demo currently uses a TypeScript reimplementation; a WASM port from hw3/Wordrange/AVL.cpp is on the roadmap.

assets.title: HW2 — N-Queens (real C++ → WASM)
assets.description: Place N queens on an N×N board such that no two attack each other. The Board / Queens classes from the original CSE 101 homework are compiled to WebAssembly — the only thing JavaScript adds is a step-driver and the canvas renderer. Watch the backtracker place queens column-by-column with each conflict and unwinding step animated.

assets.title: HW3 — AVL Wordrange
assets.description: Self-balancing BST of strings. Insert words and watch the tree rotate (LL/RR/LR/RL) to maintain balance. Range queries highlight every node lexicographically between two endpoints.
